mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate earnings prospects weaken.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts indicates expanding supply-side capacity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns as corporate margins stabilize.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 for any 3-year window, or 5-day returns decline despite concurrent INDPRO/HOUST growth.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising inflation expectations (CPIAUCSL) lead to higher real interest rates, which depress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain positive in 70%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL rises and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) signals robust demand conditions, which drive corporate earnings and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are negative despite GDPC1 growth above 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates a tightening labor market, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 60%+ of periods with PAYEMS growth above 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal strong demand conditions, which correlate with positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for any 3-year window, or 20-day returns decline despite RSAFS/HOUST growth.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL increase exceeding 0.5% signals rising inflation, which prompts central bank tightening and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL rises > 0.5% monthly.
